title: Math Vocabulary
description: Key math terms, definitions, and formulas

cards.back: A whole number (positive, negative, or zero) with no fractional part

cards.front: A whole number with no fractional part
cards.back: Integer
A number greater than 1 whose only factors are 1 and itself

cards.front: A number whose only factors are 1 and itself
cards.back: Prime number
A number greater than 1 that has factors other than 1 and itself
A number that can be expressed as a fraction p/q where q is not zero
A number that cannot be expressed as a simple fraction (e.g. pi, square root of 2)
The top number in a fraction
The bottom number in a fraction
The result of dividing one number by another
Quotient
The number being divided
The number you divide by
The amount left over after division
The result of multiplying two or more numbers
Product
The result of adding two or more numbers
The result of subtracting one number from another
A number that divides evenly into another number
The product of a number and any integer (e.g. multiples of 3: 3, 6, 9...)
The largest factor shared by two or more numbers
The smallest number that is a multiple of two or more numbers
A number that shows how many times a base is multiplied by itself
A number that, when multiplied by itself, gives the original number
The distance of a number from zero on a number line (always non-negative)
Absolute value of x
The longest side of a right triangle, opposite the right angle

cards.front: The longest side of a right triangle
cards.back: Hypotenuse
a² + b² = c² (where c is the hypotenuse)
Pythagorean Theorem
The total distance around the outside of a shape
length × width
½ × base × height
πr² (pi times radius squared)
2πr or πd (pi times diameter)
Area of a circle
Circumference of a circle
length × width × height
πr²h
The distance from the center of a circle to any point on the circle
The distance across a circle through its center (twice the radius)
The average — sum of all values divided by the number of values
The middle value when numbers are arranged in order
The value that appears most frequently in a data set
The difference between the largest and smallest values
Rise over run — the rate of change of a line (m = Δy/Δx)
Slope
Slope-intercept form (m = slope, b = y-intercept)
y = mx + b
A symbol (usually a letter) that represents an unknown value
The number multiplied by a variable (e.g. 3 in 3x)
A fixed value that does not change
A mathematical statement that two expressions are equal
A mathematical statement comparing expressions using <, >, ≤, or ≥
Lines in the same plane that never intersect (same slope)
Lines that intersect at a 90-degree angle (slopes are negative reciprocals)
An angle less than 90 degrees
An angle greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees
An angle that measures exactly 90 degrees
Two angles that add up to 90 degrees
Two angles that add up to 180 degrees
Complementary angles
Supplementary angles
A triangle with two equal sides and two equal angles
A triangle with three equal sides and three 60° angles
A triangle with no equal sides
A closed figure with three or more straight sides
A polygon with exactly four sides
The likelihood of an event occurring, expressed as a number between 0 and 1
An arrangement of items where order matters
A selection of items where order does not matter
Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication, Division, Addition, Subtraction (order of operations)
PEMDAS — Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication/Division, Addition/Subtraction
